steps.title: Identify what drives adoption
steps.content: The researchers found that performance expectancy — the perceived benefit of the service — positively influences the intention to adopt premium versions. This is the key signal: perceived benefit directly increases adoption intention.

steps.title: Apply the signal to each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: Overstates the research. The study showed performance expectancy positively influences adoption intention, not that adoption is unaffected by all other factors. ✗
  • Choice B: Confuses the scope of the study. The research did not compare brand loyalty to Spotify versus other streaming services. ✗
  • Choice C: Contradicts the study's own methodology. The researchers used user statements about perceived usefulness as a valid measure of performance expectancy — they treated those statements as meaningful, not suspect. ✗
  • Choice D: Follows directly from the finding. If perceived benefit drives adoption intention, companies should try to enhance how beneficial users perceive the premium service. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
